Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex A sophisticated cyber attack targeting transportation systems through multi-stage infiltration, leveraging spearphishing, privilege escalation, and ICS sabotage to disrupt critical infrastructure operations.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disrupt transportation networks and compromise industrial control systems

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: Gather public-facing infrastructure details (e.g., IP ranges, SCADA system vendors) for transportation networks through open-source intelligence collection.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: Establish command-and-control servers in geographically distributed cloud environments to mask the attack origin.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2013-2597 [CWE-502]: Microsoft Office Remote Code Execution
stage.description: Deliver weaponized Excel macros via spearphishing emails to privileged users, exploiting CVE-2013-2597 to execute arbitrary code on compromised endpoints.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Deploy backdoors using PowerShell commands executed through exploited vulnerabilities in custom API interfaces.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: Create scheduled tasks in shared directories to maintain persistent access across system reboots.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.technique_name: Exploitation of Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.description: Exploit unpatched ICS software vulnerabilities to escalate privileges to administrative levels.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Inject malicious code into legitimate transportation management processes to evade detection by security tools.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: Extract plaintext credentials from domain controllers using LSASS memory dumps to obtain administrative access.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Enumerate network shares to map internal topology and identify vulnerable ICS/SCADA subsystems.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Use stolen credentials to move laterally across segmented networks via RDP to access core transportation control nodes.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: Steal sensitive operational data (plans, configurations) and compress it into archives for exfiltration.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Encrypt and exfiltrate stolen data via covert DNS queries to external command servers.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Trigger ICS malfunctions by injecting malicious code into control systems, causing traffic signal disruptions or disabling emergency brakes in autonomous vehicles.

Scenario.summary: Operation Sharpshooter executed a multi-stage attack against transportation systems, beginning with spearphishing to gain initial access, escalating privileges through ICS vulnerabilities, and ultimately disrupting critical infrastructure operations through industrial control system sabotage.
